get_store()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    session = await store.get_session(request.session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n    else:\n        session_id = request.session_id\n\n    # Register surface\n    surface_id = await store.register_surface(\n        session_id,\n        request.surface_type\n    )\n\n    return {\n        \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n        \"session_id\": session_id\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/surfaces/heartbeat\")\nasync def Self-Modification endpoints\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback\")\nasync def api_v1_process_feedback(request: FeedbackRequestModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Process user feedback for self-modification or component iteration.\n\n    Returns a diff for approval or confirmation of application.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    # Map string to enum\n    feedback_type_map = {\n        \"self_modification\": FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n        \"component_iteration\": FeedbackType.COMPONENT_ITERATION,\n        \"routing_correction\": FeedbackType.ROUTING_CORRECTION,\n        \"behavior_adjustment\": FeedbackType.BEHAVIOR_ADJUSTMENT,\n    }\n\n    feedback_type = feedback_type_map.get(\n        request.feedback_type,\n        FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION\n    )\n\n    feedback_req = FeedbackRequest(\n        feedback=request.feedback,\n        feedback_type=feedback_type,\n        context=request.context,\n        session_id=request.session_id,\n        require_approval=request.require_approval\n    )\n\n    response = await _feedback_processor.process_feedback(feedback_req)\n\n    return {\n        \"status\": response.status,\n        \"message\": response.message,\n        \"confidence\": response.confidence,\n        \"artifact_name\": response.artifact_name,\n        \"artifact_type\": response.artifact_type.value if response.artifact_type else None,\n        \"approval_id\": getattr(response, 'approval_id', None),\n        \"diff\": {\n            \"artifact_name\": response.diff.artifact_name,\n            \"artifact_type\": response.diff.artifact_type.value,\n            \"change_summary\": response.diff.change_summary,\n            \"confidence\": response.diff.confidence,\n            # Don't include full before/after in response to save space\n            # Client can fetch separately if needed\n        } if response.diff else None\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback/approve\")\nasync def api_v1_approve_change(request: ApprovalRequest):\n    \"\"\"Approve   if not _component_library:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Component library not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    updated = def api_v1_iterate_component(component_id: str, request: ComponentIterateRequest):\n    \"\"\"Iterate a component based on user feedback.\"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return This module maps each cluster to\n+its ArgoCD API base URL and an `access` mode, loaded from\n+``config/clusters.yaml`` and hot-reloaded via an mtime-checked cache — the same\n+strategy every hot-reloaded config artifact uses (stat the file each call,\n+re-read only when its mtime changed).\n+\n+The fetch strand holds no ArgoCD credentials, so it can only *consume*\n+``read-only-proxy`` endpoints. Any cluster that is unmapped, or mapped to an\n+access mode the strand cannot satisfy (``authenticated`` with no credential\n+path), resolves as **unsatisfiable** — the ArgoCD source then fails with an\n+honest ``fetch_coverage`` caveat. This is what makes querying the wrong\n+instance impossible: a wrong-instance query would otherwise return not-found\n+(indistinguishable from \"app doesn't exist\") and silently drop the sync row.\n+\n+See docs/plan/plan.md → Fetch Strand → Cluster→ArgoCD Endpoint Resolution.\n+\"\"\"\n+\n+from dataclasses import dataclass\n+from logging import getLogger\n+from pathlib import Path\n+from typing import Optional\n+\n+import yaml\n+\n+logger = getLogger(__name__)\n+\n+# config/clusters.yaml lives at the repo root under config/; this file is\n+# src/fetch/clusters.py, so the repo root is three parents up.\n+CLUSTERS_PATH = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent / \"config\" / \"clusters.yaml\"\n+\n+# The only access mode the fetch strand can satisfy: no credentials needed,\n+# a proxy injects a read-only token.\n+CONSUMABLE_ACCESS = \"read-only-proxy\"\n+\n+# mtime-checked cache (mirrors src/components/hot_reload.py's pattern).\n+_cache: Optional[dict] = None\n+_cache_mtime: Optional[float] = None\n+\n+\n+def _read_clusters_file(path: Path = CLUSTERS_PATH) -> dict:\n+    \"\"\"Parse the raw YAML, returning the ``clusters:`` mapping (or {}).\"\"\"\n+    try:\n+        raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text()) or {}\n+    except FileNotFoundError:\n+        logger.warning(f\"clusters config not found at {path}; all clusters unmapped\")\n+        return {}\n+    except (yaml.YAMLError, OSError) as e:\n+        logger.error(f\"Failed to parse clusters config {path}: {e}\")\n+        # Preserve the previously-known-good cache rather than poisoning it:\n+        # return whatever is cached (possibly empty) so a malformed edit does\n+        # not flip every mapped cluster to unmapped mid-flight.\n+        return _cache or {}\n+    if not isinstance(raw, dict):\n+        logger.error(f\"clusters config {path} is not a mapping (got {type(raw).__name__})\")\n+        return _cache or {}\n+    clusters = raw.get(\"clusters\", {})\n+    if not isinstance(clusters, dict):\n+        logger.error(f\"'clusters' in {path} is not a mapping (got {type(clusters).__name__})\")\n+        return _cache or {}\n+    return clusters\n+\n+\n+def get_clusters(force: bool = False) -> dict:\n+    \"\"\"\n+    Return the parsed ``clusters`` mapping, reloading only when the file's\n+    mtime has changed (or when ``force`` is set, for tests).\n+\n+    A benign parse failure returns the last good cache instead of an empty\n+    dict, so a transiently-malformed edit never silently unmaps everything.\n+    \"\"\"\n+    global _cache, _cache_mtime\n+    try:\n+        mtime = CLUSTERS_PATH.stat().st_mtime\n+    except OSError:\n+        # File vanished: keep the last-known-good cache (best effort).\n+        return _cache or {}\n+\n+    if force or _cache is None or mtime != _cache_mtime:\n+        parsed = _read_clusters_file()\n+        # Don't poison the cache on a parse failure: _read_clusters_file\n+        # already returns the prior cache in that case, so this is a no-op\n+        # when parsing failed — but guard explicitly for clarity.\n+        _cache = parsed\n+        _cache_mtime = mtime\n+        logger.debug(f\"Reloaded clusters config ({len(parsed)} clusters) from {CLUSTERS_PATH}\")\n+    return _cache\n+\n+\n+def reset_cache() -> None:\n+    \"\"\"Clear the mtime cache (test hook).\"\"\"\n+    global _cache, _cache_mtime\n+    _cache = None\n+    _cache_mtime = None\n+\n+\n+@dataclass(frozen=True)\n+class ArgocdResolution:\n+    \"\"\"Result of resolving a cluster to an ArgoCD endpoint.\n+\n+    ``satisfiable`` is True only when the strand can actually issue the query —\n+    i.e. the cluster is mapped AND its access mode is consumable without\n+    credentials. When False, ``reason`` explains why (for the caveat strip).\n+    \"\"\"\n+\n+    cluster: Optional[str]\n+    argocd_api: Optional[str]\n+    access: Optional[str]\n+    satisfiable: bool\n+    reason: Optional[str] = None\n+\n+\n+def resolve_argocd_endpoint(cluster: Optional[str]) -> ArgocdResolution:\n+    \"\"\"\n+    Resolve a cluster name to a consumable ArgoCD API base URL.\n+\n+    Resolution rules (every unsatisfiable case carries a human-readable\n+    ``reason`` for the fetch_coverage caveat):\n+\n+    * ``cluster`` is None/empty → unsatisfiable (\"no cluster configured …\")\n+    * cluster absent from clusters.yaml → unsatisfiable (\"no ArgoCD mapping …\")\n+    * access == ``read-only-proxy`` → satisfiable, argocd_api set\n+    * access == ``authenticated`` → unsatisfiable (\"requires authentication …\")\n+    * any other/unknown access mode → unsatisfiable (\"unsupported access mode …\")\n+    \"\"\"\n+    if not cluster:\n+        return ArgocdResolution(\n+            cluster=None,\n+            argocd_api=None,\n+            access=None,\n+            satisfiable=False,\n+            reason=\"No cluster configured for this project; It is passed through the router\n+(``route_utterance`` → ``process_intent`` → the fetch/synthesize/escalate\n+branches); each stage records the duration it measures, and ``process_intent``\n+persists one ``dispatch_timings`` row per intent thread via\n+``SessionStore.record_dispatch_timings(**timings.to_fields())``.\n+\n+Design notes:\n+\n+- The clock is injectable (default ``time.monotonic``) so tests get\n+  deterministic durations without sleeping.\n+- Only stages actually measured appear in ``to_fields()``; unmeasured stages\n+  are simply absent (the store upsert leaves them NULL), which is the honest\n+  representation — e.g. ``synthesize_first_token_ms`` cannot be measured until\n+  the synthesize strand streams, and ``escalate_ms`` is absent for hot-path\n+  dispatches.\n+- ``percentiles`` uses the nearest-rank method (ceil(q/100 * n)), matching how\n+  latency SLOs are typically reported. SQLite has no built-in percentile, so\n+  the store fetches ordered per-stage values and calls into here.\n+\"\"\"\n+\n+import time\n+from typing import Callable, Optional\n+\n+\n+Clock = Callable[[], float]\n+\n+# The timing stages tracked per dispatch. Mirrors the dispatch_timings columns\n+# (minus intent_id/created_at). Kept here as the canonical list the collector\n+# accepts so a typo in a stage name is a KeyError, not a silent no-op.\n+DISPATCH_TIMING_STAGES = (\n+    \"router_ms\",\n+    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n+    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n+    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n+    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n+    \"escalate_ms\",\n+    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n+    \"stt_ms\",\n+    \"first_render_ms\",\n+)\n+\n+\n+class DispatchTimings:\n+    \"\"\"Accumulates per-stage millisecond durations for one dispatch.\n+\n+    Usage in the router::\n+\n+        timings = DispatchTimings()\n+        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n+\n+        fetch_start = timings.clock()\n+        ...\n+        timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n+\n+        await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n+    \"\"\"\n+\n+    def __init__(self, clock: Clock = time.monotonic):\n+        self._clock = clock\n+        self._durations: dict[str, int] = {}\n+\n+    @property\n+    def clock(self) -> Clock:\n+        \"\"\"The monotonic clock callable (exposed so callers share one clock).\"\"\"\n+        return self._clock\n+\n+    def record(self, stage: str, ms: int | float | None) -> None:\n+        \"\"\"Record a stage duration in ms. None is ignored (stage stays absent).\"\"\"\n+        if ms is None:\n+            return\n+        if stage not in DISPATCH_TIMING_STAGES:\n+            raise KeyError(f\"Unknown dispatch timing stage: {stage!r}\")\n+        self._durations[stage] = int(ms)\n+\n+    def elapsed_ms(self, start: float, end: float | None = None) -> int:\n+        \"\"\"Milliseconds between ``start`` and ``end`` (default: now), via the clock.\"\"\"\n+        return int(((end if end is not None else self._clock()) - start) * 1000)\n+\n+    def to_fields(self) -> dict[str, int]:\n+        \"\"\"Return the recorded stages as a kwargs dict for record_dispatch_timings.\"\"\"\n+        return dict(self._durations)\n+\n+    def get(self, stage: str) -> Optional[int]:\n+        \"\"\"Return a recorded stage duration, or None if unmeasured.\"\"\"\n+        return self._durations.get(stage)\n+\n+\n+def percentiles(values: list[int], qs: tuple[int, ...] = (50, 95)) -> dict[int, int]:\n+    \"\"\"Nearest-rank percentiles for a list of integer samples.\n+\n+    Each ``q`` (0..100) maps to ``values[ceil(q/100 * n) - 1]`` after sorting\n+    ascending, clamped to ``[0, n-1]``. Returns ``{q: value}``. Raises\n+    ``ValueError`` on an empty list — callers should skip empty stages rather\n+    than call this (the store does so).\n+    \"\"\"\n+    if not values:\n+        raise ValueError(\"percentiles() requires at least one value\")\n+    ordered = sorted(values)\n+    n = len(ordered)\n+    out: dict[int, int] = {}\n+    for q in qs:\n+        if not 0 <= q <= 100:\n+            raise ValueError(f\"percentile q must be in [0, 100], got {q}\")\n+        idx = (q * n + 99) // 100 - 1  # ceil(q/100 * n) - 1\n+        idx = max(0, min(n - 1, idx))\n+        out[q] = ordered[idx]\n+    return out\ndiff --git a/src/intent/router.py b/src/intent/router.py\nindex 4ce9d04..45281c5 100644\n--- a/src/intent/router.py\n+++ b/src/intent/router.py\n@@ -7,6 +7,7 @@ Uses LLM to classify intents by type and project, then routes:\n \"\"\"\n import asyncio\n import json\n+import time\n import uuid\n from dataclasses import dataclass\n from enum import Enum\n@@ -17,6 +18,7 @@ from typing import Any, Optional\n from ..components.hot_reload import get_reload_manager\n from ..escalate.handler import EscalateRequest, escalate_intent\n from ..escalate.llm import get_zai_client, ModelClass\n+from ..instrument.timings import DispatchTimings\n from ..session.store import get_store\n from ..fetch.commands import FetchRequest, FetchContext, IntentType as FetchIntentType\n from ..fetch.orchestrator import execute_fetch\n@@ -57,6 +59,10 @@ class RoutedIntent:\n     classification: IntentClassification\n     session_id: str\n     utterance: str\n+    # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n+    # (one LLM call per utterance) and shared across every intent thread from\n+    # that utterance — see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md.\n+    router_ms: int | None = None\n \n \n # Path to the router segmentation prompt.
